commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram prompt-context projection so hydrated reply-chain media paths override `media_ref` in structured chat context, with a regression assertion update.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +1. Total +19 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main projects node.mediaRef into structured context even when the reply chain already has a hydrated mediaPath. I did not execute the harness in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should require live Telegram proof before merge; no automated repair is needed because no blocking code defect was found.

target.title: fix(telegram): expose hydrated media refs in prompt context
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes Telegram reply-image prompt context for #87401.

- Telegram reply-chain context can retain `telegram:file/<file_id>` as plugin-internal metadata, but model-facing structured conversation context now uses the hydrated local media path when reply media has already been resolved.
- The image tool no longer receives an unsupported Telegram pseudo-URI for the cached reply image path covered by this flow.
- The change is intentionally scoped to Telegram prompt-context projection; it does not teach the core image tool to understand Telegram-specific file IDs.
- Reviewers should focus on the Telegram reply-media handoff in `extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts` and the regression in `extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`.

## Linked context

Closes #87401

Related media-store/image-tool work: #87024, #87101.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Requested from the local Codex maintainer workflow for #87401.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram reply-chain photo context no longer exposes an unsupported `telegram:file/<file_id>` reference to the model/image-tool path when the photo has already been downloaded and hydrated.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw source worktree, Node/Vitest runtime, Telegram handler test harness with mocked Telegram `getFile` and media fetch, and temporary OpenClaw state/media storage created by the test harness.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts -t 'hydrates bot reply targets cached from earlier Telegram replies'`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts src/media/media-reference.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): focused regression passed with `1 passed | 71 skipped`; full Telegram bot suite passed with `72 passed`; message-cache/image-tool/media-reference regression set passed with `176 passed`; autoreview reported `clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.
- Observed result after fix: structured Telegram conversation context uses the hydrated `ReplyChain` media path for the cached reply image and explicitly does not expose `telegram:file/generated-photo-1`; the reply-chain cache still preserves Telegram's internal file id metadata.
- What was not tested: live Telegram Desktop/Bot API behavior was not exercised in this PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proof uses the existing local Telegram test harness rather than a live Telegram account; it verifies the download/hydration and prompt-context handoff that caused the issue.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the focused regression failed before the fix because the structured context still contained `media_ref: "telegram:file/generated-photo-1"` instead of the hydrated media path.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts -t 'hydrates bot reply targets cached from earlier Telegram replies'`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts src/media/media-reference.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts -t 'hydrates bot reply targets cached from earlier Telegram replies'` after rebasing onto current `origin/main`
- `git diff --check`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Updated the Telegram bot reply-target hydration regression to assert model-facing structured context receives the hydrated media path and not `telegram:file/generated-photo-1`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The focused regression failed before the production change with `media_ref: "telegram:file/generated-photo-1"` in structured conversation context.

If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Telegram image reply context exposed to the agent changes from an unsupported Telegram pseudo-URI to an image-tool-readable local media path when hydrated media is available.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Prompt-context shape for Telegram reply-chain media.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The change only replaces `media_ref` when the existing Telegram reply-media hydration path has already produced a concrete media path for the same message id; otherwise it preserves the existing cached `mediaRef` fallback.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Draft PR is ready for maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI and any maintainer-requested live Telegram proof.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Local autoreview was clean; no PR review comments have been addressed yet.
